diagrams.description: Criminal Law defines offenses against society and prescribes punishment. This flowchart maps its conceptual structure: the ultimate goal is to maintain social order and deliver justice by prohibiting harmful conduct. It integrates core elements—actus reus (criminal act) and mens rea (guilty mind)—with classification of offenses, followed by defenses (justifications and excuses). The process flows through criminal procedure (arrest, trial, sentencing) protected by constitutional safeguards (due process, presumption of innocence), and culminates in outcomes like punishment, rehabilitation, and public safety.

Presumption of innocence, due process,
right to counsel, protection against
